mission.title: Install and Configure Devfile on Kubernetes
mission.description: Devfile is a Kube-native API for cloud development workspaces specification, enabling developers to define and manage development environments in Kubernetes. Installing Devfile allows you to leverage its CRDs and schemas for creating and managing cloud development workspaces efficiently.

steps.title: Clone the Devfile repository
steps.description: Clone the Devfile API repository to your local machine to access the necessary manifests and CRDs.

steps.title: Apply the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
Deploy the CRDs required for Devfile by applying the manifest files. This will create the necessary resources in your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/crds/devworkspace.devfile.io_devworkspaces_crd.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/crds/devworkspace.devfile.io_devworkspacetemplates_crd.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the CRDs are installed
steps.description:
Check that the CRDs have been successfully installed by listing them.
```bash
kubectl get crds | grep devworkspace
```

steps.title: Deploy the Devfile API
steps.description:
Apply the deployment manifest to set up the Devfile API in your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/deployment.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the Devfile API deployment
steps.description:
Ensure that the Devfile API pods are running correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default | grep devfile
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Devfile API deployment to optimize resource usage.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment devfile-api --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Devfile API deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the deployment of the Devfile API to clean up the resources.
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/deployment.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Clean up the CRDs that were created during the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/crds/devworkspace.devfile.io_devworkspaces_crd.yaml
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/crds/devworkspace.devfile.io_devworkspacetemplates_crd.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current CRDs and deployment configurations to backup before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get crds -o yaml > devfile_crds_backup.yaml
kubectl get deployment devfile-api -o yaml > devfile_api_backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the CRDs and deployment
upgrade.description:
Apply the new version of the CRDs and deployment manifest for the Devfile API.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/crds/devworkspace.devfile.io_devworkspaces_crd.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/crds/devworkspace.devfile.io_devworkspacetemplates_crd.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devfile/api/v2.3.0/deploy/deployment.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded Devfile API pods are running correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default | grep devfile
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Devfile API pods are not starting, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name>
``` Look for any error messages that indicate configuration issues or missing resources.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive errors about CRDs not being found, ensure that they were applied correctly. Re-run the CRD apply commands and verify with:
```bash
kubectl get crds | grep devworkspace
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are being killed due to out of memory (OOM), consider increasing the memory limits set during post-install configuration. Update the resource limits with:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment devfile-api --limits=memory=1Gi
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Devfile will have the necessary CRDs and the Devfile API deployment running in your Kubernetes cluster. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are in a 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: kubectl get pods -n default | grep devfile

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
